sales.title:
JUDAEA. Time of Agrippa II, ca. 50-100 C.E AE 20mm (7.90 gms), Caesarea Panias (as Neronias) Mint, year 14 of Nero (67/8 C.E.). NGC Ch VF, Strike: 3/5 Surface: 3/5.
Meshorer-128; Hendin-6309; RPC-I, 4850 (as a Sepphoris civic issue). In the names of Roman emperor Nero and procurator Vespasian. Obverse: Large SC; Reverse: Legend and date in five lines; all within wreath. A RARE and intriguing type, this example features the name of the current Roman emperor, Nero, in what would be the final year of his reign along with the name of a future emperor, Vespasian, who would come to subjugate Judaea shortly thereafter.
From the Rabbi Victor H. Weissberg and Tamar Weissberg Collection.
To view all items from the Rabbi Victor H. Weissberg and Tamar Weissberg Collection, click here.
